== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1602|1602]] – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1848|1848]] – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:658|658]] – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1348|1348]] – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 11 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月11日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 11일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 11 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月十一]][[zh:十一月十一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 11 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月11日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 11일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 11 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月十一]][[zh:十一月十一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 11 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月11日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 11일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 11 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月十一]][[zh:十一月十一]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q840338]] Wikidata name: 11th day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 11|qq=November 11}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月十一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 11일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 11''' is the 11th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:784|784]] – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1477|1477]] – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1795|1795]] – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1814|1814]] – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.
== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1602|1602]] – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1848|1848]] – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:658|658]] – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1348|1348]] – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:784|784]] – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1477|1477]] – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1795|1795]] – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1814|1814]] – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.
== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1602|1602]] – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1848|1848]] – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:658|658]] – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1348|1348]] – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).

== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:784|784]] – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1185|1185]] – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1477|1477]] – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1795|1795]] – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1814|1814]] – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.

== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:643|643]] – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:658|658]] – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1348|1348]] – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月十一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 11일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 11''' is the 11th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|784|en|784|WD=Q30083|lt=784}} – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}} – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477}} – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1795|en|1795|WD=Q7815|lt=1795}} – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1814|en|1814|WD=Q6943|lt=1814}} – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.
== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1602|en|1602|WD=Q6727|lt=1602}} – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}} – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1849|en|1849|WD=Q7650|lt=1849}} – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|658|en|658|WD=Q30176|lt=658}} – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1348|en|1348|WD=Q6085|lt=1348}} – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|784|en|784|WD=Q30083|lt=784}} – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}} – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477}} – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1795|en|1795|WD=Q7815|lt=1795}} – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1814|en|1814|WD=Q6943|lt=1814}} – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.
== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1602|en|1602|WD=Q6727|lt=1602}} – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}} – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1849|en|1849|WD=Q7650|lt=1849}} – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|658|en|658|WD=Q30176|lt=658}} – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1348|en|1348|WD=Q6085|lt=1348}} – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|784|en|784|WD=Q30083|lt=784}} – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}} – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477}} – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1795|en|1795|WD=Q7815|lt=1795}} – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1814|en|1814|WD=Q6943|lt=1814}} – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.
== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1602|en|1602|WD=Q6727|lt=1602}} – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}} – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1849|en|1849|WD=Q7650|lt=1849}} – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|658|en|658|WD=Q30176|lt=658}} – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1348|en|1348|WD=Q6085|lt=1348}} – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|10|11|12|10|11|12|11|1111|qq=}}

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月十一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 11일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 11''' is the 11th day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643|1=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a royal family member, was attacked by Soga no Iruka and committed suicide. * In the 3rd year of the Enryaku era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|784|en|784|WD=Q30083|lt=784|1=784}} – December 27): Emperor Kanmu relocated the capital from Heijō-kyō to Nagaoka-kyō. * In the 1st year of the Bunji era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185|1=1185}} – December 4): Emperor Go-Shirakawa issued an imperial decree ordering the subjugation of Minamoto no Yoshitsune. * In the 9th year of the Bunmei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477|1=1477}} – December 16): Daikichi Masahiro withdrew his troops from Suō Province, effectively ending the Ōnin War. * In the 6th year (intercalary 11th month) of the Kansei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1795|en|1795|WD=Q7815|lt=1795|1=1795}} – January 1): Following the incident known as the "Shii Incident," Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu’s protégé, Ōtsuki Genzan, celebrated the New Year according to the solar calendar (the Dutch New Year) for the first time in Japan. * In the 11th year of the Bunka era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1814|en|1814|WD=Q6943|lt=1814|1=1814}} – December 22): A priest from Okayama, Kurozumi Sōchū, founded the Kurozumi religion.
== Births == * In the 6th year of the Keichō era (leap month) ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1602|en|1602|WD=Q6727|lt=1602|1=1602}} – January 4): Tōdō Takatsugu, lord of the Tsushima Domain, was born (died 1676). * In the 12th year of the Bunsei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} – December 6): Yūri Kōsei, a politician who drafted the original version of the Five-Article Oath, was born (died 1909). * In the 1st year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848|1=1848}} – December 6): Kawakami Sōroku, an officer in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1899). * In the 2nd year of the Kaei era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1849|en|1849|WD=Q7650|lt=1849|1=1849}} – December 25): Nogi Maresuke, a general in the Imperial Japanese Army, was born (died 1912). * In the 2nd year of the Bunkyū era ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862|1=1862}} – December 31): Watarase Shōzaburō, a zoologist, was born (died 1929).
== Deaths == * In the 2nd year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|643|en|643|WD=Q31634|lt=643|1=643}} – December 30): Prince Yamase Ōe, a member of the royal family and a child of Prince Shōtoku, died. * In the 4th year of Emperor Saimei’s reign ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|658|en|658|WD=Q30176|lt=658|1=658}} – December 11): Prince Arima, a child of Emperor Tenmu, died. * In the 4th year of the Teiwa era / 3rd year of the Shōhei era ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1348|en|1348|WD=Q6085|lt=1348|1=1348}} – December 2): Emperor Hanazono, the 95th Emperor of Japan, died (born 1297).
